title: bahiopsis subincisa
intro: Bahiopsis subincisa, also known as the Mexican daisy or yerba de la tormenta, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating wounds and skin irritations. Bahiopsis subincisa is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used as a topical application for its medicinal properties.
